turns.content: man I am in a good mood today
Good to hear! What's got you fired up? Something happen at the shop, or is this a personal win?
finally pulled the trigger on a new truck. got a 24 Ram 1500. traded in the Ford

Nice! The 2024 Ram 1500 is a great truck. The ride quality on those is hard to beat — that coil spring rear suspension makes it feel more like driving a luxury vehicle than a pickup.

What trim did you go with? And how did the trade-in value come back on the Ford?
got the Big Horn trim. didnt need the Laramie or anything crazy. the Ford trade in was decent, they gave me 22 for it which is about what I expected

Big Horn is the sweet spot — you get the good interior, the 8.4-inch touchscreen, heated seats, all the stuff that matters without paying the premium for leather and chrome trim you don't need. Practical choice.

$22K on the trade is solid for a used F-150 in this market. Did you finance the rest, or did you have some cash set aside for it?
financed most of it but got a decent rate. 4.9 percent for 60 months. could be worse

4.9% for 60 months is actually pretty reasonable right now. Rates have been ugly the last couple years, so under 5% is a solid deal. You didn't overpay for the money.

Is this going to be your daily driver and shop truck, or do you keep things separate?
daily driver. I dont haul parts in my personal truck. got a shop van for that. this one stays clean

Smart man. Nothing worse than buying a nice truck and immediately filling the bed with greasy equipment and brake dust. Keep the shop work to the van and let the Ram stay nice.

What color did you go with?
granite crystal. the dark gray. looks sharp I aint gonna lie

Granite Crystal is one of the best colors Ram offers — it's dark enough to look sharp but doesn't show every speck of dust like black does. Good pick for someone who doesn't want to be washing it every other day.

How does it drive compared to the Ford? Notice a big difference?
brother the ride is night and day. the Ford was getting rough. this thing rides like a cloud. the 5.7 Hemi has plenty of power too

The Hemi is a proven engine — strong, reliable, and it sounds great. And yeah, the Ram ride quality is one of those things where once you feel the difference, you can't go back. That multilink coil spring rear suspension versus the Ford's leaf springs is a huge upgrade for daily driving.

Enjoy the new truck. You earned it.
appreciate it man. been wanting to do this for a while. felt good to finally make it happen. alright I better get back to it
Congrats again. Nothing wrong with rewarding yourself when the time is right. Take care of that Ram and it'll take care of you. Have a good one.
